system.description: After being subjected to Wonder's reality-bending magic, you have begun to understand how madness can be made real. You are slowly figuring out how to make the weirdest ideas manifest through Wonder's impossible architecture. Once per Scene, you can spend 1 Soma to attempt to activate the mad magic of Wonder for your own purposes. State something crazy that you want to accomplish, such as 'I want to walk on walls and ceiling', 'I want my arm to stretch out of proportion to reach that ledge', 'I want to shrink down to fit through that tiny door', 'I want doors to appear where there are only walls', or 'I want time to flow backwards for a moment'. The Storyteller will ask you to make a Position Check with Opposition Level determined by the madness of your request: **OL Easy 3** for minor impossibilities (walking on walls, glowing in the dark), **OL Medium 5** for moderate madness (stretching limbs, changing size), **OL Hard 7** for major reality warping (creating doors, manipulating time, phasing through walls). If you succeed, you gain a temporary Descriptor representing your declared impossibility for the rest of the Scene. If you fail, you have spent your Soma in vain and nothing happens. The effect immediately ends if someone makes it clear that what you are doing is utter madness—doubt and disbelief shatter the effect.
system.requirement: Experience Wonder's Gift 'Place of Pure Madness' at least once—be teleported randomly and suffer a side effect (Shrunk, Enlarged, or Confused) after attempting to alter Wonder's structure.
system.notes: This is one of the most creative and open-ended Gifts in Broken Tales. It allows Hunters to temporarily wield Wonder's reality-bending power for themselves. The 1 Soma cost per attempt encourages strategic use. The scaling OL (3/5/7 based on madness level) lets Storytellers balance power—asking to glow = easy, asking to fly = hard. The temporary Descriptor lasts entire Scene, making successful uses very powerful. The 'shattering through disbelief' mechanic is crucial: if NPCs or allies point out the impossibility ('You can't walk on the ceiling, that's insane!'), the effect ends. This encourages Hunters to act confidently and not draw attention to the madness. Clever Hunters will use this to: access unreachable areas (stretch arms, shrink size), bypass obstacles (phase through doors, walk on ceilings), surprise enemies (invisible, extra limbs), or even manipulate Wonder itself (create doors, freeze time briefly). The Storyteller should encourage creative uses but apply the OL fairly—minor utility = Easy, combat advantages = Medium, reality warping = Hard. This Gift stops working outside Wonder—it's tied to the bedlam's magic. It represents the Hunter beginning to internalize Alice's philosophy: reality is what perception makes it.
